changes.before: The road north of Hearthglen had quit pretending to be a road.
changes.after: North of Hearthglen, the road had broken down into ruts and shoulder.

changes.before: The lead was no longer miles. It was ovens.
changes.after: The lead was measured in ovens now.

changes.before: Bells thin in the damp air, meant to order a morning, not warn a wall.
changes.after: Bells thinned through the damp air, the sound of an ordinary morning.

changes.before: Habit still ran the place, and habit had always been the plague's ally.
changes.after: People still moved by routine there. That made them easy for the plague.

changes.before: Habit still ran the place, and habit had always been the plague's ally.
changes.after: People still moved by routine there. That made them easy for the plague.

changes.before: Habit still ran the place, and habit had always been the plague's ally.
changes.after: People still moved by routine there. That made them easy for the plague.

changes.before: Her knees almost folded.
changes.after: Her knees buckled.

changes.before: Sacks stood in ranks almost to the loft.
changes.after: Sacks stood in ranks under the loft.

changes.before: The room gave one last drag against the staff, then let go.
changes.after: The pull in the grain dragged once more against the staff, then broke.

changes.before: The yard outside killed the victory at once.
changes.after: Jaina lost the victory the moment she saw the yard outside.

changes.before: Jaina almost laughed.
changes.after: A raw sound caught in Jaina's throat and never became a laugh.

changes.before: All around them, the city kept confessing.
changes.after: All around them, proof kept piling up: warm rolls under cloth, half a loaf on an old man's knee, bread cooling on a board behind an open kitchen door.

changes.before: All around them, the city kept confessing.
changes.after: All around them, proof kept piling up: warm rolls under cloth, half a loaf on an old man's knee, bread cooling on a board behind an open kitchen door.

changes.before: All around them, the city kept confessing.
changes.after: All around them, proof kept piling up: warm rolls under cloth, half a loaf on an old man's knee, bread cooling on a board behind an open kitchen door.

changes.before: All around them, the city kept confessing.
changes.after: All around them, proof kept piling up: warm rolls under cloth, half a loaf on an old man's knee, bread cooling on a board behind an open kitchen door.

changes.before: Not one more store. Not one more lane.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: She could strike a third site and save one street, perhaps two.
changes.after: She could strike a third site and save one street. At most two.

changes.before: The city had already answered her.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: The city had already answered her.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: The ride back through the service roads went faster because Jaina had stopped hunting for one more fix.
changes.after: They drove back through the service roads hard.

changes.before: The wall, the prince, the city all held on the lip of decision.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: The wall, the prince, the city all held on the lip of decision.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: He might already have known the shape of the answer.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: Underneath it sat the real question. Stripped bare. Without him having to say what he already knew he would do. Without making her choose the words for him.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: Underneath it sat the real question. Stripped bare. Without him having to say what he already knew he would do. Without making her choose the words for him.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: There was nothing left to soften.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: There was nothing left to soften.
changes.after: [deleted]

changes.before: Whatever he had hoped to hear died so fast in his face it almost resembled relief.
changes.after: Whatever he had hoped to hear died in his face and left only blankness.

changes.before: Jaina stood with the city at her back and ruined grain on her hands and knew, with a clarity uglier than anything Kel'Thuzad had ever laid before her, that being right had brought them exactly this far and no farther.
changes.after: Jaina stood with the city at her back and ruined grain on her hands.
